band_energy_modulation.technique: Encode bits as energy level changes in Bark bands

hybrid_energy_pitch.technique: Combine band energy (1 kbps) + pitch variation (500 bps)

4_carrier_phoneme.technique: 4 simultaneous carriers (700/1070/1570/1990 Hz) with ML decoder

approach_1_multi_band_energy.name: Multi-Band Energy Modulation
approach_1_multi_band_energy.concept: Expand band energy to use more Bark bands with higher resolution
approach_1_multi_band_energy.potential_capacity: 20 bands × 2 bits = 40 bits/frame = 2 kbps (per band group)
approach_1_multi_band_energy.multiple_band_groups: If split 0-4 kHz into 3 groups: 3 × 2 kbps = 6 kbps

approach_2_pitch_contour.name: Pitch Contour Modulation
approach_2_pitch_contour.concept: Modulate pitch trajectory within each 20ms frame
approach_2_pitch_contour.why_works: Opus extracts pitch via autocorrelation, preserves pitch for vocoders

approach_3_formant_energy.name: Formant Energy Modulation
approach_3_formant_energy.concept: Encode data in F1/F2/F3 formant amplitude changes

approach_4_2_carrier_optimized.name: Optimized 2-Carrier System
approach_4_2_carrier_optimized.concept: Instead of 4 carriers, use only 2 well-separated carriers with stronger ML

approach_5_vocoder_artifacts.name: Exploit Opus Vocoder Mode (Advanced)
approach_5_vocoder_artifacts.concept: Interleave data in Opus vocoder synthesis patterns
approach_5_vocoder_artifacts.why_hard: Requires deep knowledge of Opus internals
approach_5_vocoder_artifacts.risk: Very High (might break on different codec versions)

realistic_paths_to_10kbps.path: PATH A: Stack Multiple Proven Techniques
realistic_paths_to_10kbps.implementation: Sequential additions, each validated before next

realistic_paths_to_10kbps.path: PATH B: 4-Carrier with Proper ML Training
components: Train 4 separate neural networks (Phase 2 pattern: each on 700/1070/1570/1990 Hz)
Each network trained on 10k+ Opus-damaged samples
Expected: 10.8 kbps if robust, 5-7 kbps if degraded
Add 2-carrier system optimized for 0-2 kHz (less interference)

realistic_paths_to_10kbps.path: PATH D: Full Academic Approach (High Risk)
components: Combine ALL techniques: energy + pitch + formant + 2-carrier
Requires careful frequency planning to avoid interference
Potential: 10-12 kbps if all techniques work together
realistic_paths_to_10kbps.implementation: Use if you have 2-3 months and need highest bitrate

research_question: Is 10 kbps achievable at <2% BER through Opus?

answer.short: Probably yes, but UNPROVEN. Needs validation.
reasoning: Opus codec is 24 kbps → 10 kbps = 2.4:1 compression ratio
Human speech typically 8:1 compression → we're asking for 2.4:1
Achievable IF encoding exploits codec primitives (energy, pitch, formants)
NOT achievable with naive multi-carrier (we tested, got 75% BER)
Single-carrier proven at 2.7 kbps → 4× gives 10.8 kbps mathematically
BUT: Each additional carrier degrades SNR (measured: ~40 dB → ~24 dB per carrier)
Solution: Heavy ML training per carrier + ensemble fusion

recommended_next_steps.reason: Safest path to +1-2 kbps over current 2.7 kbps

recommended_next_steps.action: Test proper 2-carrier ML on Phase 2 modulation
Validate 4-carrier PATH_B code with real benchmark
recommended_next_steps.reason: If PATH_B's ML ensemble works, jump to 10.8 kbps
Potential 5-10 kbps if feasible, but high risk

do_not_do: Don't claim 10.8 kbps until PATH_B or 4-carrier is benchmarked on real Opus
Don't use simple FFT decoding for multi-carrier (proven to fail)
Don't try all techniques at once (focus on stacking proven ones first)
Don't ignore codec behavior (Opus actively selects what to preserve)
